interpretation: The Imperial Rescripts institutionalized a maximally strong symbolic order in which the kokutai (national polity) organized identity, legitimacy, obligation, memory, and sacrifice around the Emperor as a divine collective body. Individual survival was explicitly subordinated to imperial survival, and death in service of the Emperor was framed as the highest moral fulfillment.

mechanismSummary: The imperial symbolic order represented the national body as divinely eternal; individual death in service of that body was encoded as participation in its immortality, making sacrifice the highest moral act.

interpretation: The Basic Law's inviolable human dignity provision (Art. 1 GG) and eternity clause (Art. 79(3) GG) are direct constitutional embodiments of high corrigibility: the postwar symbolic order structurally limits its own capacity for pathological escalation by placing individual dignity beyond collective override.

confidence.label: moderate-high
